note: Energy and carbon estimates are rough orders of magnitude. Cloud figures use provider-reported or MLCommons-style inference energy per 1k tokens and include PUE. Local figures use GPU/CPU TDP and assumed utilization. Real-world values vary with model size, quantization, batching, cooling, and hardware generation.

cloud_providers.name: OpenAI GPT-4o
cloud_providers.notes: High-quality frontier model; energy per token is higher due to dense attention and larger parameter count.

cloud_providers.name: Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet
cloud_providers.notes: Frontier model with strong reasoning; generation energy dominates for long outputs.

cloud_providers.name: OpenAI GPT-4o-mini
cloud_providers.notes: Small, efficient model. Best carbon and cost profile for high-volume simple tasks.

cloud_providers.name: Google Gemini 1.5 Flash
cloud_providers.notes: Flash tier optimized for throughput and lower per-token energy.

cloud_providers.name: Local / self-hosted (general estimate)
cloud_providers.notes: Approximate for a 70B-class model on a single high-end GPU with good utilization.

local_hardware.name: RTX 4090 workstation
local_hardware.notes: Single RTX 4090 plus supporting CPU, RAM, and PSU losses.

local_hardware.name: RTX 5090 workstation
local_hardware.notes: Next-gen flagship; higher TDP but better perf-per-watt.

local_hardware.name: Mac Mini M4 Pro 24GB
local_hardware.notes: Compact ARM SoC; excellent perf-per-watt for small models.

local_hardware.name: Dual RTX 3090 rig
local_hardware.notes: Common miner/AI repurposed rig. High idle draw from multiple GPUs.

local_hardware.name: Cloud GPU rental (A100 80GB)
local_hardware.notes: Data-center A100; PUE and shared host overhead already included in provider footprint.

local_hardware.name: Cloud GPU rental (H100 80GB)
local_hardware.notes: Hopper generation; very fast inference but high TDP.
